About.title: ABOUT US
About.definition: Salsa Rayo is a dance school in Agios Dimitrios, Athens, Greece, specializing in Cuban Salsa and Bachata. Founded in 2025 by instructors Konstantinos Bitsis and Anna Lontou, we offer beginner and intermediate classes Monday through Thursday. With monthly packages from €50 to €99 and a welcoming community atmosphere, Salsa Rayo makes learning Latin dance accessible to everyone.
About.schoolDescription:
Salsa Rayo dance school was born from a shared dream. Konstantinos and Anna met in 2018, as teenagers with a passion for dance and the desire to create something of their own.

Starting as a dance couple, they traveled to many festivals around the world, from Europe to New York. They also participated in Salsa competitions, where they won awards and distinctions, both in duets and group performances. With a strong stage presence and unique chemistry, Konstantinos and Anna evolved into a dynamic duo that authentically conveys their love for dance, both to students and audiences.

In 2025, the dream becomes reality. Salsa Rayo opens its doors in Agios Dimitrios, as a space where teaching meets passion and technique combines with inspiration.

The name Rayo, which means lightning in Spanish, was not chosen by chance. It represents strength, speed, and energy - elements that characterize both their style and the school's philosophy. Their goal is to share their knowledge and experience, inspire new dancers, and transmit the culture of Salsa and Bachata in a space full of creativity and respect for the art of dance.
About.annaDescription:
I started dancing at the age of 6, with modern dance as my primary training. Since 2016, I have dedicated myself to Salsa and Bachata, two dances that deeply express me and have led me to evolve both as a dancer and as a teacher.

By frequently traveling and attending classes and workshops from renowned teachers in Greece and abroad, I continuously enrich my knowledge and influences, elements that are reflected both in my technique and the energy I bring to my classes. Additionally, I love choreographing and being on stage, conveying through movement my passion and vitality for dance.

My teaching combines positive energy with strict technical guidance, emphasizing proper foundation and personal expression. My special love is for "Movement and Styling" classes that cultivate confidence, approaching solo style in a more authentic and personalized way.
About.konstantinosDescription:
I am a dance teacher specializing in Salsa, a dance full of vitality, passion, and explosiveness. My relationship with dance began at a very young age, but my introduction to Salsa started at 16, when I discovered it in my small town. I discovered the magic not only of rhythm and movement, but also the way it can be taught, with enthusiasm, laughter, and respect for each student.

In my journey so far, I have had the opportunity to travel to various countries and learn from the best salsa teachers in the world. This experience has helped me evolve and convey to my students technique, style, and the passion that makes dance truly come alive.

At our school, I ensure that from the first lesson, a warm and friendly environment is created so that students feel comfortable. At the same time, I want them to have the opportunity to evolve further and reach the next level, maintaining a professional character in the advanced classes.

Orishas.title: Orishas in Salsa Dance
Orishas.subtitle: Discover the Sacred Rhythms
Orishas.intro: In salsa, the Afro element is deeply incorporated through Orisha movements from Yoruba traditions. Below we showcase some of the existing Orishas - the ones that we dance to. Each deity brings specific rhythms and dance characteristics, connecting spiritual expression with vibrant salsa energy.
Orishas.bottomText: In our salsa classes, we honor these traditions by incorporating Orisha-inspired movements, helping you connect with the deeper cultural roots of Afro-Cuban dance while mastering the rhythms that move both body and spirit.

Footer.description: Your gateway to the magical world of social dancing! Join our passionate community and discover the joy of salsa and bachata.

Pricing.pageSubtitle: Choose the perfect plan for your dance journey
Pricing.contactUs: Contact us for personalized guidance on selecting the right package for your dance goals.

Faq.pageSubtitle: Everything you need to know about Salsa Rayo
Faq.q3: What dance styles do you teach?
Faq.a3: We teach Salsa, Bachata, Mambo, Pachanga, Afro-Fusion and Boogaloo. Each style is offered at multiple levels so you can progress at your own pace.
Faq.q4: How do I know which level is right for me?
Faq.a4: If you're completely new to dance, start with Salsa Beginners, which is the first level. If you already have some experience, reach out to us and we'll help you find the right class.
Faq.q5: What days and times are classes held?
Faq.a5: Classes run Monday through Thursday, from 19:00 to 23:00. Check our schedule section on the homepage for the full weekly timetable.
Faq.q6: What should I wear to class?
Faq.a6: Wear comfortable clothing that allows freedom of movement. Dance shoes with suede soles are ideal. If you don't have any, don't worry, you can simply wear socks. Sneakers are unsuitable because they restrict movement and can cause injury.
Faq.a7: Each class lasts 50 minutes, and there is a 10-minute break between classes.
Faq.q8: Can I try a class before committing to a package?
Faq.a8: Yes! We want you to feel confident before you commit. Contact us to arrange a trial class so you can experience the energy, the teaching style, and the community firsthand.
Faq.a9: We offer three flexible monthly packages: Rayo 8 (2 classes per week, €50/month), Rayo 16 (4 classes per week, €75/month), and Rayo 24 (6 classes per week, €99/month). Each package gives you access to the full range of class styles.
Faq.a10: Yes! We offer a 10% discount for students with a valid student ID. Simply present your student ID when signing up and the discount will be applied to any of our packages.
Faq.a12: We're located at Thermopylon 19, Agios Dimitrios, Athens. The area is easily accessible by public transport as well as by car. There is easy parking on the surrounding streets.
Faq.q13: How do I get there by public transport?
Faq.a13: Agios Dimitrios is connected by the Athens Metro (Line 2 - Red Line). The school is a short walk from the Agios Dimitrios metro station. You can also reach us by bus — check our map for detailed directions.
Faq.a14: Our teachers are Konstantinos Bitsis, Anna Lontou and Natasha Chumakova, award-winning salsa dancers with international competition experience. They've trained with top instructors worldwide and bring years of experience, passion and positive energy to every class.
Faq.q15: What does 'Rayo' mean and what is Salsa Rayo?
Faq.a15: "Rayo" means lightning in Spanish. Salsa Rayo is a dance school founded by Konstantinos Bitsis and Anna Lontou — an internationally competing dance duo. The name represents the explosive energy, speed, and power we bring to every class. We combine world-class salsa technique with a warm, welcoming atmosphere in Athens.
Faq.stillHaveQuestionsText: Can't find the answer you're looking for? We'd love to hear from you.

hero.title: Salsa vs Bachata
hero.subtitle: Two incredible Latin dances, one big question — which one should you learn first?

quickAnswer.title: The Quick Answer
quickAnswer.text: Both salsa and bachata are amazing dances that will transform your social life, fitness, and confidence. The best choice depends on your personality and what draws you to dance. Here is everything you need to know to decide — or better yet, learn both!

whatIsSalsa.title: What Is Salsa?
whatIsSalsa.text: Salsa is an energetic partner dance with roots in Cuban Son, Afro-Cuban rhythms, and New York mambo. Danced to infectious 4/4 time music with congas, timbales, and brass, salsa is all about timing, turns, and connection. Dancers move in circular patterns with dynamic partner exchanges, cross-body leads, and expressive body isolations. The energy is electric — fast footwork, sharp spins, and an unmistakable sense of joy fill every social dance floor.

whatIsBachata.title: What Is Bachata?
whatIsBachata.text: Bachata is a romantic partner dance born in the Dominican Republic. Set to slower, guitar-driven melodies with a distinctive "derecho" bass rhythm, bachata emphasizes close connection, smooth hip movement, and emotional expression. The basic step is a simple side-to-side pattern with a signature tap on the fourth beat. Modern bachata incorporates sensual body waves and intricate turn patterns while staying grounded in its intimate, heartfelt origins.

comparison.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
comparison.bachataDifficulty: Easier to start — simpler basic step

noPartner.title: Do I Need a Partner?
noPartner.text: This is the number one question we hear — and the answer is a firm NO. At Salsa Rayo, we rotate partners in every class so you dance with everyone. You will learn to lead or follow with multiple partners, which actually makes you a better dancer faster. Come alone, bring a friend, or bring a partner — everyone is welcome.

learnBoth.title: Can I Learn Both?
learnBoth.text: Absolutely — and we highly recommend it! Many of our students take both salsa and bachata classes. The dances complement each other beautifully: salsa sharpens your timing and footwork while bachata deepens your body movement and connection. Together, they make you a more complete, versatile dancer. At most social events, DJs play both salsa and bachata, so knowing both means you never sit out a song.

cta.title: Ready to Start Dancing?
cta.text: Whether you choose salsa, bachata, or both — your dance journey starts here. Join Salsa Rayo in Athens and discover the magic of Latin dance.

story.title: Our Story
story.p1: Salsa Rayo was born from the shared dream of two passionate dancers. Konstantinos Bitsis and Anna Lontou met in 2018 — teenagers with a fire for dance and a vision to build something of their own.
story.p2: What started as a dance partnership quickly became something bigger. Together they traveled to festivals across Europe and New York, competing in salsa competitions and earning awards in both duo and group performances. Their stage presence, chemistry, and authenticity set them apart.
story.p3: In 2025, the dream became reality. Salsa Rayo opened its doors in Agios Dimitrios, Athens — a space where world-class technique meets heartfelt passion, and where every student is treated like family.
story.p4: The name "Rayo" — meaning lightning in Spanish — was chosen to represent strength, speed, and explosive energy. These are the qualities that define both their dance style and the school's philosophy.

konstantinos.name: Konstantinos Bitsis
konstantinos.bio: Konstantinos discovered salsa at 16 in his small hometown and immediately knew it would shape his life. Since then, he has trained with the world's top salsa instructors across multiple countries, building a deep technical foundation combined with infectious energy and warmth.
konstantinos.philosophy: His teaching philosophy centers on creating a welcoming environment from the very first class while maintaining professional standards in advanced levels. He believes every student deserves both comfort and challenge — a space to feel at ease and the push to reach the next level.
konstantinos.imageAlt: Konstantinos Bitsis - Co-Founder of Salsa Rayo

anna.name: Anna Lontou
anna.bio: Anna has been dancing since age 6, starting with modern dance before dedicating herself fully to Salsa and Bachata in 2016. She continuously enriches her skills by traveling to workshops and classes with renowned instructors around the world. Her love for choreography and stage performance shines through in every class.
anna.philosophy: Anna's teaching combines positive energy with rigorous technical guidance, emphasizing strong foundations and personal expression. Her specialty is Movement and Styling classes that build confidence and cultivate an authentic, personalized solo style.
anna.imageAlt: Anna Lontou - Co-Founder of Salsa Rayo

studio.title: Our Studio
studio.text: Located at Thermopylon 19, Agios Dimitrios, our studio is easily accessible by Athens Metro (Line 2 — Red Line, Agios Dimitrios station) and by car with convenient street parking. The space was designed with dancers in mind — a professional-grade floor, mirrors, and a sound system that brings every rhythm to life.
studio.imageAlt: Salsa Rayo dance studio in Agios Dimitrios, Athens

philosophy.title: Our Teaching Philosophy
philosophy.levelsText: Clear progression from beginner to advanced, so you always know your next step.
philosophy.techniqueText: We believe strong foundations and genuine enjoyment go hand in hand — never one without the other.
philosophy.communityText: Dance is social. We foster a warm, inclusive environment where friendships form naturally on and off the floor.
philosophy.rotationText: We rotate partners in every class. No partner needed — and you will become a better dancer by dancing with everyone.

cta.title: Join Our Community
cta.text: Ready to start your dance journey? Get in touch and take your first step onto the dance floor.

hero.title: Salsa Classes in Athens
hero.subtitle: Learn Cuban Salsa at Salsa Rayo in Agios Dimitrios. From your very first step to advanced technique, our classes will have you dancing with confidence and joy.

social.title: Social Connection
social.description: Salsa is the ultimate social dance. Meet new people, build friendships, and become part of a vibrant worldwide community.

fitness.title: Full-Body Fitness
fitness.description: Burn up to 400 calories per hour while having fun. Salsa improves coordination, balance, posture, and cardiovascular health.

cultural.title: Cultural Richness
cultural.description: Dive into the Afro-Cuban roots of salsa. Understand the rhythms, the history, and the cultural traditions behind every movement.

fun.title: Pure Joy
fun.description: There is nothing quite like the feeling of dancing salsa to live music. The energy, the rhythm, the connection — it is addictive in the best way.

classes.title: Our Salsa Classes
classes.subtitle: We offer structured levels so you always know your next step. Each class is 50 minutes with a 10-minute break between sessions.

level1.title: Salsa Level 1
level1.description: Perfect for complete beginners. Learn the basic step, timing, fundamental turns, and partner connection. No experience needed.

level2.title: Salsa Level 2
level2.description: For dancers with solid basics. Expand your repertoire with more complex turn patterns, styling, and musicality.

mambo.title: Mambo
mambo.description: Explore the roots of salsa through Mambo. Focus on body movement, rhythm interpretation, and the classic New York style.

techniques.title: Salsa Techniques
techniques.description: Refine your footwork, spins, and body isolations. A technique-focused class for dancers who want to level up their skills.

konstantinos.name: Konstantinos Bitsis
konstantinos.bio: Award-winning salsa dancer who has trained with the world's top instructors. Konstantinos brings technical precision, infectious energy, and a warm teaching style to every class.

anna.name: Anna Lontou
anna.bio: Dancing since age 6, Anna combines rigorous technique with positive energy. She specializes in body movement, styling, and helping dancers find their personal expression.

location.title: Our Studio
location.address: Thermopylon 19, Agios Dimitrios 17341, Athens. 5 min from Agios Dimitrios Metro (Line 2).

q1.question: Do I need dance experience to start salsa?
q1.answer: No! Our Salsa Level 1 class is designed for complete beginners with no prior dance experience. We start from the very basics.

q2.question: Do I need to bring a dance partner?
q2.answer: No, you don't need a partner. In our classes, we rotate partners so everyone dances with everyone. Come alone or with friends!

q3.question: How much do salsa classes cost?
q3.answer: Monthly packages range from €50 (8 classes/month) to €99 (24 classes/month). Student discounts are available.

q4.question: What should I wear to salsa class?
q4.answer: Wear comfortable clothes that allow movement. Dance shoes with suede soles are ideal, or just wear socks. Avoid sneakers.

cta.title: Start Your Salsa Journey
cta.subtitle: Join our welcoming community and discover the magic of Cuban Salsa. Your first class is just a click away.

hero.title: Bachata Classes in Athens
hero.subtitle: Discover the art of Bachata at Salsa Rayo. From sensual basics to expressive body movement, our classes in Agios Dimitrios will have you dancing with confidence.

connection.title: Connection & Intimacy
connection.description: Bachata is one of the most expressive partner dances. Learn to communicate through movement and build deep connection with your partner.

accessible.title: Accessible for Beginners
accessible.description: With its simple basic step and slower tempo, Bachata is the perfect entry point into Latin dance. You'll be dancing from day one.

community.title: Global Community
community.description: Join a worldwide community of Bachata dancers. Social dancing events, festivals, and congresses await you across the globe.

expression.title: Expression & Musicality
expression.description: Develop your musical interpretation and personal style. Bachata encourages creativity and emotional expression in every step.

level1.title: Bachata Level 1
level1.description: Perfect for beginners with no prior dance experience. Build a solid foundation in Bachata basics.

points.0: Basic step, timing, and rhythm fundamentals
points.1: Lead and follow techniques for partner connection

level2.title: Bachata Level 2
level2.description: For dancers with foundational knowledge looking to expand their repertoire and styling.

konstantinos.name: Konstantinos Bitsis
konstantinos.bio: With years of experience in Latin dance and training from world-renowned instructors, Konstantinos brings technical precision and infectious energy to every Bachata class.

anna.name: Anna Lontou
anna.bio: Anna's passion for movement and styling shines through her Bachata teaching. Her classes emphasize body movement, musicality, and personal expression.

days.title: Class Days
days.description: Bachata classes run on Monday and Thursday evenings, 19:00-21:00. Check our full schedule for all available time slots.

address.title: Our Studio
address.description: Thermopylon 19, Agios Dimitrios 17341, Athens, Greece. A modern, air-conditioned studio with professional dance flooring.

transport.title: Getting Here
transport.description: 5 minutes walk from Agios Dimitrios Metro Station (Line 2). Bus lines 550, A2, and B2 stop nearby. Free street parking available.

rayo8.name: Rayo 8
rayo8.description: 8 classes per month — 2 classes per week. Perfect for getting started with Bachata.

rayo12.name: Rayo 12
rayo12.description: 12 classes per month — 3 classes per week. Mix Bachata with other dance styles.

rayo16.name: Rayo 16
rayo16.description: 16 classes per month — 4 classes per week. Our most popular package for dedicated dancers.

0.question: Is bachata hard to learn as a beginner?
0.answer: Not at all! Bachata is actually one of the most beginner-friendly Latin dances. The basic step is simple, and our Level 1 class builds your confidence from day one.

1.question: Do I need a dance partner for bachata?
1.answer: No partner needed! We rotate partners in class so everyone practices with different people. Come alone or bring friends.

2.question: What's the difference between bachata and salsa?
2.answer: Bachata is slower and more romantic, danced in a closer embrace. Salsa is faster and more energetic. Many students learn both — they complement each other beautifully.

3.question: How often should I take bachata classes?
3.answer: We recommend at least 2 classes per week for steady progress. Our Rayo 16 package (€75/month) gives you 4 classes per week across all styles.

cta.title: Start Your Bachata Journey
cta.subtitle: Join our welcoming community and discover the joy of Bachata. Your first class is just a click away.
